invariantResults.evidence: New content uses 4-space indentation consistent with rest of page.
Filename 'delete-pages.md' is lowercase, hyphenated, and reflects the page title.
Page title 'Delete pages' does not end with '-ing'.
Numbered list has 5 steps (within the 8-step limit).

Pronouns in updated content have clear antecedents within the same sentence. Line 71: 'its own' refers to 'child page'; 'the parent page's' refers to the parent page's permission settings (elliptical possessive); 'that page' refers to 'child page'.
Permission requirement (a restriction) is presented in an {% info %} callout, which is appropriate.
Line 71: 'child pages that have their own permission settings' correctly uses 'that' for a restrictive clause.
Permission names 'Read', 'Delete' are bolded consistently: **Read**, **Delete**.
No underlines in new content.
No inappropriate all-caps usage in new content.
Punctuation after bold text is not bolded (e.g., period after '**page permissions**' on line 19).
No headings were modified. Existing headings use sentence case.
Single spaces between sentences. Line 71: '...permission settings. If a child page...' uses one space.
Line 19: '**Read** and **Delete**' has only two items; no serial comma needed.
No semi-colons in new content.
No em dashes in new content.
No dashes requiring '--' conversion in new content.
New content is task-oriented: tells users what permissions they need to accomplish the delete task.
Page title 'Delete pages' uses sentence case.
Page title 'Delete pages' uses imperative mood verb phrase for a scenario/how-to page.
No 'How to' prefix in page title.
No new lists added. Existing ordered list for step-by-step instructions is appropriate.
New content is concise. Info callout is a single sentence. Paragraph in 'Deleting a page with child pages' is two focused sentences.
Both additions lead with the key information (what permissions are required).
No 'please' in new content.
All new sentences use active voice. Line 19: 'You need...' Line 71: 'You also need...' and 'you need to have...'
All new content uses present simple tense ('need', 'uses', 'need to have').
American English used throughout. No British spellings detected.
Terminology is consistent: 'page permissions' (plural for multiple) on line 19, 'page permission' (singular for one) on line 71. Both correctly match quantity context.
Possessive 'its' used on line 71 ('its own permission settings') with clear antecedent 'child page' in the same sentence. Not the standalone pronoun 'it'.
New content uses indicative mood ('You need', 'uses').
Language is appropriate for business-user persona. Attempt 2 replaced technical 'inherit permissions' with 'uses its own permission settings', which is clearer for business users.

Line 71 uses non-contracted forms ('does not' removed in rephrasing; 'need to' used throughout). Note: pre-existing contraction 'there's' on line 38 creates a page-level inconsistency, but this is not introduced by the writer.
No interaction verbs in new content (content is informational, not instructional steps).

PREVIOUSLY FAILED (attempt 1). Line 71 now reads 'you need to have the **Delete** permission set directly on that page' — 'must' has been replaced with 'need to'. No instances of 'must' directed at people remain anywhere in the file (confirmed via full-file search). Consistent with line 19 ('You need the **Read** and **Delete**...') and line 55 ('you need to delete its variants').
Line 71: 'If a child page uses its own permission settings instead of the parent page's, you need to have...' correctly leads with the condition before the instruction.
{% info %} callout used for additional context (permission requirements). Info is the correct severity level — not a warning (no destructive action) or tip (not optional).
Info callout (lines 17-21) is a single sentence; the exception for 'single-sentence callouts where the title would be redundant' applies.
No callout type name ('Info') written inside the info callout.
Callout type 'info' is used correctly for supplementary context about permissions.
No 'have to' used in new content.
Line 71: 'its own' correctly uses possessive 'its' (not contraction 'it's'). 'parent page's' correctly uses possessive apostrophe-s.
Cross-references link to permissions_pagelevel_xp, providing centralized access to permission information.
New content documents current product state (permission model). No migration guides or breaking changes mentioned.
No 'Next steps' section added.
Page remains independent. Permission details are cross-referenced via page_link rather than duplicated.
Permission note works for both new and returning users. Links to permission management page for fuller context.
No modifications to _guides collection.
No modifications to _api files.
Article structure intact: Title ('Delete pages'), Introduction ('To delete a page:'), Body (steps 1-5), Result (line 51: 'You have successfully deleted the page.').
Introduction 'To delete a page:' provides brief context before the steps.
New content mentions permissions as prerequisites, which is appropriate per this invariant's guidance to 'mention prerequisites if necessary.'
Result paragraph (line 51) remains intact: 'You have successfully deleted the page.'
New cross-references use {% page_link %} Liquid tag (not raw Markdown links).
YAML front matter present between --- delimiters (lines 1-13).
All required front matter fields present: title ('Delete pages'), identifier ('XofWCQ'), order (800), redirect_from ('x/XofWCQ'), persona ('business'), license (1).
redirect_from includes 'x/XofWCQ' matching the identifier.
license: 1 (standard tier).
Info callout uses valid Liquid syntax: {% info %} with matching {% endinfo %} (lines 17, 21). Blank lines around content inside callout.
Filename 'delete-pages.md' uses kebab-case and reflects page title 'Delete pages'.
license: 1 (standard tier) set correctly.
redirect_from is present and includes 'x/XofWCQ'.

Both page_links use valid syntax: {% page_link permissions_pagelevel_xp linkText="page permissions" %} and {% page_link permissions_pagelevel_xp linkText="page permission" %}. Collection attribute omitted, consistent with all other page_links on this page.
Identifier 'permissions_pagelevel_xp' follows the expected naming convention (snake_case + _xp suffix). Actual existence verification deferred to accuracy-reviewer.
Link text is descriptive: 'page permissions' and 'page permission' clearly describe the linked content. No vague phrases like 'click here'.

codesamples.reason: No code samples in the changes. Task adds prose permission notes only.

metaKnowledgeResults.evidence: page_link identifier 'permissions_pagelevel_xp' follows the expected naming convention (snake_case + _xp suffix). Identifier verification against frontmatter is the accuracy-reviewer's scope.

antiPatternResults.evidence: No dashes in new content. No em dash, en dash, or double-hyphen issues.
This is an update-crossrefs task, not a create task.
Style-review scope; acceptance criteria enumeration is task-planner's domain.

additionalFindings.evidence: All headings are h2 (##). No skipped levels. Hierarchy: 'Redirects from deleted pages' (h2, line 59), 'Deleting a page with child pages' (h2, line 67), 'Restore deleted pages' (h2, line 79).
New {% info %} callout (lines 17-21) follows the same pattern as the existing {% info %} callout (lines 53-57) — single-sentence content with blank lines inside the tag.
New cross-references use {% page_link %} Liquid syntax, consistent with all other internal links on the page.
All Liquid tags in new content are syntactically valid: {% info %}/{% endinfo %} pair and {% page_link %} tags with correct attribute format.
New prose is clear, concise, and scannable. Info callout is one sentence. Paragraph in 'Deleting a page with child pages' is two focused sentences with a conditional structure.

Attempt 2 rephrasing of line 71 is well-constructed. 'uses its own permission settings instead of the parent page's' is an elliptical possessive (short for 'the parent page's [permission settings]') — grammatically correct and idiomatic. The possessive apostrophe in 'page's' is properly placed. Sentence parallelism is maintained: both clauses on line 71 now use 'need' consistently ('You also need...' and 'you need to have...').

regressionCheck.note: All 63 invariants that passed in attempt 1 continue to pass. INV-style-132 now also passes, bringing total to 64/64.
